content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PnacrrcES CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
Decenrber 28,2A04
Michelle Le
UST Training Group
P.O. Box 581241
Elk Grove, CA95758
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. l-04-259
Dear Ms. Le:
This leffer is in response to your request on for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').I Since your request seeks
general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
As a Registered Environmental Health Specialist with the San Joaquin County
Environmental Health Department whosejob duties include inspection ofunderground
storage tank systems, would you have a conflict of interest under the Act by accepting
employment as a "designated operator" for underground storage tank facilities in other
counties?
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit public officials from maintaining outside employment
during their te,nure as public officials. However, any income receivd may be subject to
the reporting and conflict of interest provisions of the Act.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18-997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 I 14; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)


File No. I-04-259
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are currently ernployed as a Registered Environrnental Health Specialist with
the San Joaquin county Environmental Health Department ("SJCEHD'). Yourjob
duties include inspections of underground storage tank systems ("UST's').
A new California regulation requires all UST facilities to obtain a designated
operator by January 1,2005. A designated operator is an individual who has passed the
California UST System Operator exrun, which is administered by the International Code
Counsel. The designated operator responsibilities include performing a monthly UST
inspection, veriffing annual testing is done on time, and annually training of facility
personnel. UST owners have the option of becoming the designated operator themselves
orhiring an outside company.
You would like to offer your services as a designated operator in counties other
than the one in which you are employed. You would not perform the role of designated
operator for anyone who owns facilities in the county in which you are employed.
The State Water Resources Control Bomd ("SWRCB') has a website posting the
names and contact information for persons offering their senrices as a designated
operator. SWRCB will not add an individual to this posting who is employed as an
inspector until a 'lrriffen notice" is received from the Commission stating that a conflict
of interest does not exist.
You have attached the memorandum from the SWRCB to local agencies
informing "local a3errcy staff' of the potential for conflicts of interest should they also
provide services as designated UST operators. Additionally, the memorandum states the
SWRCB's requirement that SWRCB will not add a local agency staffperson to its list of
available designated UST operators unless it receives "a written determination from the
FPPC stating that there is no conflict of interest between that individual's performing his
or her job duties as a local agency staffand his or her job duties as a Designated UST
Operator."
ANALYSIS
The Act does not bar public officials from maintaining outside employment
during their te,lrure as public officials.3 (Evans Advice Letter, No. I-01-l4i). However,
the Act does requirepublic officials to rqrort, in certain circumstances, any sources of
outside income." (Section 973lz,regulation 18730.) Additionally, the Act's conflict-of-
interest provisions prohibit any public official from making participating in making, or
3 Our advice in this letter is limited to the provisiors of the Political Reform Act. There may be
other restrictions placed on outside emplolment under any incompatible activities policy adopted by your
agency. The Commission cannot offer advice in that area. Any questions regarding incompatible activities
should be addressed by the San Joaquin County Counsel.
a Designated employees under an agency's conflict ofinterest code are required to report each
source of income aggregating five hundred dollars or more in value, from certain identified sources, and a
general description of the business activity of each source. (Section B7ZO7.)


File No. I-04-259
Page No. 3
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a govemmental decision in which
the ofificial has a financial interest. (Section 87100, regulation 1g700.)
A public official has a disqualifring financial interest in a governmental decision
within the meaning of section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's economic interests. The
economic interests that may give rise to a potential conflict of interest are defined in
section 87103 and regulations 18703 through 18703.5 and include any source of income,
includingpromised income, to thepublic official which aggregates to$500 ormore
within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c), regulation 13703.3.) Since
your inquiry is limited to potential conflicts ofinterest arising from outside income, and
you have not identified any other potential economic interests, our discussion is limited to
potential conflicts of interests arising fiom a source of income economic interest.
Section 82030(a) provides:
"(a) 'Income' means ... a payment received, including but not
limited to any salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent,
proceeds from any sale, gift, including any grft of food or
beveragq loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received
by the filer, reimbursement for expenses, per diem, or contribution
to an insurance or pe,nsion program paid by any person other than
an employer, and including any communitypropertyinterest in the
income of a spouse. Income also includes an outstanding loan.
Income of an individual also includes aproratashare of any
income of anybusiness entityortrust in which the individual or
spouse owns, directlS indirectly or benefici ally, a lO-percent
interest or greater. 'Income,' other than a gft, does not include
income receivedfrom any source outside the jurisdiction and not
doing business within the jurisdtetion, not planntng to do business
within the jurisdtction, or not having done business within the
iurisdiction during the two years prior to the time any statement or
other action is required under this title." (Emphasis added.)
Section 82035 defines "jurisdiction- urith respect to a local govemment agency as:
"the region, county, city, district or other geographical area in which it has jurisdiction.,,
Since you are employed by the County of San Joaquin, the jurisdiction applicable
to you is the county. You have indicated that you will only offer your sernices in
counties other than San Joaquin, and that you will not perform thi role of designated
operator for anyone who owns facilities in the county in which you are employed. As
long as the source of your income is outside the eounty, does not do business or plan to
do business within the county, or has not done business within the county during the trvo
years prior to the time any govenrmental decision is madg the compenrutiol you receive
from that source is not considered income from purposes of the "oohi"t of interest
provisions of the Act.


File No. I-O4-259
Page No. 4
Since your request is a general one relating to prohibitions under the Act with
regard to accepting outside employment, you have not presented any facts pertaining to a
specific goveflrmental decision or to a specific source of income that may "uor" 
u
potential conflict of interest. If you find that such a situation maybepresented you may,
ofcourse, seek additional advice based on those facts.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
J. Lenkeit
Counsel, Legal Division
wJLjg
